mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns show positive mean during rate hikes with p-value less than 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.3 percentage points over three months signals deteriorating labor market conditions and impending recession, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in greater than 65 percent of instances when the unemployment threshold is breached over a 5-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous acceleration in retail sales and consumer price inflation indicates demand-pull inflationary pressure that forces central banks to tighten policy, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces positive forward returns in greater than 60 percent of triggered cases over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the index of industrial production reflects expanding manufacturing output and improving corporate revenue expectations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 during recovery phases, or 5-day returns are statistically indistinguishable from zero with t-statistic less than 1.5.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price index levels exceeding 285.0 indicate entrenched inflation that compels aggressive monetary tightening, compressing equity multiples and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 delivers positive 20-day returns in greater than 70 percent of periods where CPIAUCSL remains above the threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in total nonfarm payrolls and stable unemployment rates confirm robust economic expansion without overheating, supporting corporate earnings growth and yielding positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined labor signal correlates negatively with forward returns with Rank IC less than negative 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Increasing housing starts reflect strong consumer confidence and rising demand for construction materials and financial services, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no significant positive correlation with housing starts with t-statistic less than 1.5 across multiple market cycles.

mechanism_narrative: Declining gross domestic product growth combined with falling industrial production signals a broad economic contraction that reduces corporate profit margins and generates negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in greater than 60 percent of instances when both supply indicators decline simultaneously over a 4-year sample.
